title: Async URL Fetcher
description: Build a minimal concurrent URL fetcher using asyncio — from a sync script to gathered tasks, then split across files.

steps.title: Synchronous baseline
steps.goal: Fetch two URLs sequentially with httpx in a plain script.
steps.instructions: Start with the simplest version: a `main()` that calls a blocking `fetch` twice. This is the baseline we will make concurrent.

snippets.label: Blocking fetch script

steps.title: Async fetch coroutine
steps.goal: Replace blocking calls with `async def` and `AsyncClient`.
steps.instructions:
Convert `fetch` to a coroutine using `httpx.AsyncClient`. Drive the event loop with `asyncio.run(main())`.

Still fetch URLs one after another — concurrency comes next.

snippets.label: Async main and fetch
snippets.code:
import asyncio
import httpx

URLS = [
    "https://httpbin.org/get",
    "https://httpbin.org/delay/1",
]


async def fetch(client: httpx.AsyncClient, url: str) -> int:
    response = await client.get(url, timeout=10.0)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.status_code


async def main() -> None:
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        for url in URLS:
            status = await fetch(client, url)
            print(url, status)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

steps.title: Concurrent gather
steps.instructions: Build a list of coroutines and pass them to `asyncio.gather`. Total time should be close to the **slowest** URL, not the sum.

snippets.label: Gather all tasks
snippets.code:
import asyncio
import httpx

URLS = [
    "https://httpbin.org/get",
    "https://httpbin.org/delay/1",
]


async def fetch(client: httpx.AsyncClient, url: str) -> int:
    response = await client.get(url, timeout=10.0)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.status_code


async def main() -> None:
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        tasks = [fetch(client, url) for url in URLS]
        statuses = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
        for url, status in zip(URLS, statuses):
            print(url, status)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

steps.title: Split files and bound concurrency
steps.goal: Extract fetch logic to `fetcher.py` and cap in-flight requests from `main.py`.
steps.instructions:
Larger apps split responsibilities across files. Move the fetch helper to **`fetcher.py`**, keep orchestration in **`main.py`**, and use `asyncio.Semaphore` in main to limit concurrency — the same idea as a worker pool.

Create both files in the same directory and run `python main.py`.

snippets.label: New file — fetch helper
snippets.code:
import httpx


async def fetch(client: httpx.AsyncClient, url: str) -> int:
    response = await client.get(url, timeout=10.0)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return response.status_code

snippets.label: Wire semaphore in main
snippets.code:
import asyncio
import httpx

from fetcher import fetch

URLS = [
    "https://httpbin.org/get",
    "https://httpbin.org/delay/1",
    "https://httpbin.org/delay/1",
    "https://httpbin.org/get",
]

MAX_CONCURRENT = 2


async def fetch_bounded(
    client: httpx.AsyncClient, sem: asyncio.Semaphore, url: str
) -> int:
    async with sem:
        print("fetching", url)
        return await fetch(client, url)


async def main() -> None:
    sem = asyncio.Semaphore(MAX_CONCURRENT)
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        tasks = [fetch_bounded(client, sem, url) for url in URLS]
        statuses = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
        for url, status in zip(URLS, statuses):
            print(url, status)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

title: FastAPI CRUD Layout
description: Structure a FastAPI app with routers, schemas, and an in-memory article store.

snippets.label: app/main.py
app/schemas.py
app/routers/articles.py
snippets.code:
from fastapi import APIRouter
from app.schemas import ArticleCreate, ArticleOut

router = APIRouter(prefix="/articles", tags=["articles"])
_store: list[ArticleOut] = []
_next_id = 1


@router.get("", response_model=list[ArticleOut])
def list_articles():
    return _store


@router.post("", response_model=ArticleOut, status_code=201)
def create_article(payload: ArticleCreate):
    global _next_id
    item = ArticleOut(id=_next_id, **payload.model_dump())
    _next_id += 1
    _store.append(item)
    return item

snippets.label: app/main.py

title: Argparse Greet CLI
description: Build a small CLI with `argparse` for greeting users.

snippets.label: main.py
main.py
snippets.code:
import argparse


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Greet CLI")
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="person to greet")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    print(f"Hello, {args.name}!")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

snippets.label: main.py
main.py
snippets.code:
import argparse


def greet(name: str, shout: bool) -> str:
    msg = f"Hello, {name}!"
    return msg.upper() if shout else msg


def main() -> None:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Greet CLI")
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--count", type=int, default=1)
    parser.add_argument("--shout", action="store_true")
    args = parser.parse_args()
    for _ in range(args.count):
        print(greet(args.name, args.shout))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

title: Asyncio Task Queue
description: Process jobs with asyncio workers backed by a Queue.

snippets.label: main.py
snippets.code:
import asyncio


async def main() -> None:
    q: asyncio.Queue[str] = asyncio.Queue()
    for i in range(3):
        await q.put(f"job-{i}")
    print("queued", q.qsize())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

snippets.label: main.py
snippets.code:
import asyncio

STOP = None


async def worker(name: str, q: asyncio.Queue) -> None:
    while True:
        job = await q.get()
        if job is STOP:
            q.task_done()
            break
        print(name, "processing", job)
        await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
        q.task_done()

steps.title: Start workers
steps.instructions: Spawn two workers and wait on `queue.join()`.

snippets.label: main.py
snippets.code:
import asyncio

STOP = None


async def worker(name: str, q: asyncio.Queue) -> None:
    while True:
        job = await q.get()
        if job is STOP:
            q.task_done()
            break
        print(name, "processing", job)
        await asyncio.sleep(0.1)
        q.task_done()


async def main() -> None:
    q: asyncio.Queue = asyncio.Queue()
    workers = [asyncio.create_task(worker(f"w{i}", q)) for i in range(2)]
    for i in range(5):
        await q.put(f"job-{i}")
    for _ in workers:
        await q.put(STOP)
    await q.join()
    await asyncio.gather(*workers)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

steps.title: Producer task
steps.instructions: Add a producer coroutine that feeds the queue.

snippets.label: main.py
snippets.code:
import asyncio

STOP = None


async def producer(q: asyncio.Queue) -> None:
    for i in range(5):
        await q.put(f"job-{i}")
    for _ in range(2):
        await q.put(STOP)


async def worker(name: str, q: asyncio.Queue) -> None:
    while True:
        job = await q.get()
        if job is STOP:
            q.task_done()
            break
        print(name, job)
        q.task_done()


async def main() -> None:
    q: asyncio.Queue = asyncio.Queue()
    workers = [asyncio.create_task(worker(f"w{i}", q)) for i in range(2)]
    await asyncio.gather(producer(q), *workers)
    await q.join()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
